metadata.name: plip
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 (GPLv2)
metadata.summary: PLIP - Fully automated protein-ligand interaction profiler

modules.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
plipcmd.py - Main script for PLIP command line execution.
!python

functions.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd.process_pdb
documentation: Analysis of a single PDB file. Can generate textual reports XML, PyMOL session files and images as output.

functions.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd.download_structure
documentation:
Given a PDB ID, downloads the corresponding PDB structure.
Checks for validity of ID and handles error while downloading.
Returns the path of the downloaded file.

functions.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd.remove_duplicates
documentation:
Checks input lists for duplicates and returns
a list with unique entries

functions.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd.run_analysis
documentation: Main function. Calls functions for processing, report generation and visualization.

functions.name: plip-2.3.1.data.scripts.plipcmd.main
documentation: Parse command line arguments and start main script for analysis.

modules.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
test_basic_functions.py - Unit Tests for basic functionality.
coding=utf-8

classes.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.TestLigandSupport

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.TestLigandSupport.test_dna_rna
documentation: Test if DNA and RNA is correctly processed as ligands
Test for support of different ligands

classes.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.TestMapping

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.TestMapping.test_ids
documentation: Test if the atom IDs are correctly mapped from internal to original PDB.
Test

classes.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.setUp
documentation: Generate random data for the tests
noinspection PyUnusedLocal

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_euclidean
documentation: Tests for mathematics.euclidean

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_vector
documentation: Tests for mathematics.vector

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_vecangle
documentation: Tests for mathematics.vecangle

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_centroid
documentation: Tests for mathematics.centroid

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_normalize_vector
documentation: Tests for mathematics.normalize_vector

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_projection
documentation: Tests for mathematics.projection

methods.name: plip.test.test_basic_functions.GeometryTest.test_cluster_doubles
documentation: Tests for mathematics.cluster_doubles
Tests for geometrical calculations in PLIP

modules.name: plip.test.test_command_line
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
test_command_line.py - Unit Tests for special cases.

classes.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_empty_input_file
documentation: Input file is empty.

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_invalid_pdb_id
documentation: A PDB ID with no valid PDB record is provided.

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_invalid_input_file
documentation: A file is provided which is not a PDB file.

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_pdb_format_not_available
documentation: A valid PDB ID is provided, but there is no entry in PDB format from wwPDB

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_pdb_format_available
documentation: A valid PDB ID is provided, but there is no entry in PDB format from wwPDB

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_valid_pdb
documentation: A PDB ID with no valid PDB record is provided.

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_stdout
documentation: A PDB ID with no valid PDB record is provided.

methods.name: plip.test.test_command_line.CommandLineTest.test_help
documentation: A PDB ID with no valid PDB record is provided.
Checks special and extreme cases for input files.

methods.name: plip.test.test_salt_bridges.SaltBridgeTest.test_4yb0
documentation: test salt bridge detection for nucleic acids as part of the receptor

methods.name: plip.test.test_xml_parser.XMLParserTest.test_general_information
documentation: Test if general information is correctly parsed.

methods.name: plip.test.test_xml_parser.XMLParserTest.test_bsite_information
documentation: Test if the binding site information is correctly parsed.

methods.name: plip.test.test_xml_parser.XMLParserTest.test_interactions
documentation: Test if interaction information is correctly parsed.
Checks if the XML parser is working correctly

modules.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
test_metal_coordination.py - Unit Tests for Metal Coordination.
coding=utf-8

classes.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_1rmd
documentation:
Zinc binding sites in RAG1 dimerization domain (1rmd)
Reference: Harding. The architecture of metal coordination groups in proteins. (2004), Fig. 1a
Literature-validated cases from publication #

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_1rla
documentation:
Rat liver arginase, a binuclear manganese metalloenzyme (1rmd)
Reference: Harding. The architecture of metal coordination groups in proteins. (2004), Fig. 1b

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_1het
documentation:
Liver alcohol deshydrogenase (1het)
Reference: Harding. The architecture of metal coordination groups in proteins. (2004), Fig. 2

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_1vfy
documentation:
Phosphatidylinositol-3-phosphate binding FYVE domain of VPS27P protein (1vfy)
Reference: Harding. The architecture of metal coordination groups in proteins. (2004), Fig. 5

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_2pvb
documentation:
Pike parvalbumin binding calcium (2pvb)
Reference: Harding. The architecture of metal coordination groups in proteins. (2004), Fig. 6

methods.name: plip.test.test_metal_coordination.MetalCoordinationTest.test_2q8q
documentation:
Crystal Structure of S. aureus IsdE complexed with heme (2q8q)
Reference: Grigg et al. Heme coordination by Staphylococcus aureus IsdE. (2007)
Checks predictions against literature-validated interactions for metal coordination.

documentation: Returns the status and up-to-date entry in the PDB for a given PDB ID

functions.name: plip.exchange.webservices.fetch_pdb
documentation: Get the newest entry from the RCSB server for the given PDB ID. Exits with '1' if PDB ID is invalid.

documentation: General settings for PyMOL

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.make_initial_selections
documentation: Make empty selections for structures and interactions

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.standard_settings
documentation: Sets up standard settings for a nice visualization.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.set_custom_colorset
documentation: Defines a colorset with matching colors. Provided by Joachim.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.select_by_ids
documentation:
Selection with a large number of ids concatenated into a selection
list can cause buffer overflow in PyMOL. This function takes a selection
name and and list of IDs (list of integers) as input and makes a careful
step-by-step selection (packages of 20 by default)

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.object_exists
documentation: Checks if an object exists in the open PyMOL session.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_hydrophobic
documentation: Visualizes hydrophobic contacts.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_hbonds
documentation: Visualizes hydrogen bonds.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_halogen
documentation: Visualize halogen bonds.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_stacking
documentation: Visualize pi-stacking interactions.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_cationpi
documentation: Visualize cation-pi interactions.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_sbridges
documentation: Visualize salt bridges.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_wbridges
documentation: Visualize water bridges.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.show_metal
documentation: Visualize metal coordination.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.selections_cleanup
documentation: Cleans up non-used selections

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.selections_group
documentation: Group all selections

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.additional_cleanup
documentation: Cleanup of various representations

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.zoom_to_ligand
documentation: Zoom in too ligand and its interactions.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.save_session
documentation: Saves a PyMOL session file.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.png_workaround
documentation:
Workaround for (a) severe bug(s) in PyMOL preventing ray-traced images to be produced in command-line mode.
Use this function in case neither cmd.ray() or cmd.png() work.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.save_picture
documentation: Saves a picture

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.set_fancy_ray
documentation: Give the molecule a flat, modern look.

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.adapt_for_peptides
documentation: Adapt visualization for peptide ligands and interchain contacts

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.adapt_for_intra
documentation: Adapt visualization for intra-protein interactions

methods.name: plip.visualization.pymol.PyMOLVisualizer.refinements
documentation: Refinements for the visualization

modules.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
test_literature_validated.py - Unit Tests for literature-validated cases.
coding=utf-8

classes.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1eve
documentation:
Binding of anti-Alzheimer drug E2020 to acetylcholinesterase from Torpedo californica (1eve)
Reference: Chakrabarti et al. Geometry of nonbonded interactions involving planar groups in proteins. (2007)
Literature-validated cases from publication #

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1h2t
documentation:
Binding of methylated guanosine to heterodimeric nuclear-cap binding complex (1h2t)
Reference: Chakrabarti et al. Geometry of nonbonded interactions involving planar groups in proteins. (2007)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3pxf
documentation:
Binding of ANS to CDK2 (3pxf)
Reference: Betzi et al. Discovery of a potential allosteric ligand binding site in CDK2 (2012)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_2reg
plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1osn
documentation:
Binding of VZV-tk to BVDU-MP (2reg)
Reference: Bird et al. Crystal structures of Varicella Zoster Virus Thyrimidine Kinase. (2003)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_2w0s
documentation:
Binding of Vacc-TK to TDP (2w0s)
Reference: Caillat et al. Crystal structure of poxvirus thymidylate kinase: An unexpected dimerization
has implications for antiviral therapy (2008)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1vsn
documentation:
Binding of NFT to Cathepsin K (1vsn)
Reference: Li et al. Identification of a potent and selective non-basic cathepsin K inhibitor. (2006)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1p5e
documentation:
Binding of TBS to CDK2(1p5e)
Reference: De Moliner et al. Alternative binding modes of an inhibitor to two different kinases. (2003)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1acj
documentation:
Binding of Tacrine (THA) to active-site gorge of acetylcholinesterase (1acj)
Reference: Harel et al. Quaternary ligand binding to aromatic residues in the active-site gorge of
acetylcholinesterase.. (1993)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_2zoz
documentation:
Binding of CgmR to ethidium(2z0z)
Reference: Itou et al. Crystal Structures of the Multidrug Binding Repressor Corynebacterium
glutamicum CgmR in Complex with Inducers and with an Operator. (2010)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1xdn
documentation:
Binding of ATP to RNA editing ligase 1 (1xdn)
Reference: Deng et al. High resolution crystal structure of a key editosome enzyme from Trypanosoma brucei:
RNA editing ligase 1. (2004)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1bma
documentation:
Binding of aminimide to porcine pancreatic elastase(1bma)
Reference: Peisach et al. Interaction of a Peptidomimetic Aminimide Inhibitor with Elastase. (1995)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4rao
documentation:
Binding of (4rao)
Reference: Keough et al. Aza-acyclic Nucleoside Phosphonates Containing a Second Phosphonate Group
As Inhibitors of the Human, Plasmodium falciparum and vivax 6‑Oxopurine Phosphoribosyltransferases
and Their Prodrugs As Antimalarial Agents (2004)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4qnb
documentation:
Binding of (4qnb)
Reference:  Bhattacharya et al. Structural basis of HIV-1 capsid recognition by PF74 and CPSF6(2014)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4kya
documentation:
Binding of non-classical TS inhibitor 3 with Toxoplasma gondii TS-DHFR(4kya)
Reference:  Zaware et al. Structural basis of HIV-1 capsid recognition by PF74 and CPSF6(2014)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1n7g
documentation:
Binding of NADPH to MURI from Arabidopsis thaliana (1n7g)
Reference:  Mulichak et al. Structure of the MUR1 GDP-mannose 4, 6-dehydratase from Arabidopsis thaliana:
implications for ligand binding and specificity(2002)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4alw
documentation:
Binding of benzofuropyrimidinones compound 3 to PIM-1 (4alw)
Reference:  Tsuhako et al. The design, synthesis, and biological evaluation of PIM kinase inhibitors.(2012)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3o1h
plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3thy
documentation:
Binding of ADP tp MutS(3thy)
Reference:  Shikha et al. Mechanism of mismatch recognition revealed by human MutSβ bound to unpaired DNA loops.(2012)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3tah
documentation:
Binding of BGO to an an N11A mutant of the G-protein domain of FeoB.(3tah)
Reference:  Ash et al. The structure of an N11A mutant of the G-protein domain of FeoB.(2011)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3r0t
documentation:
Binding of protein kinase CK2 alpha subunit in with the inhibitor CX-5279 (3r0t)
Reference:  Battistutta et al. Unprecedented selectivity and structural determinants of a new class of protein
kinase CK2 inhibitors in clinical trials for the treatment of cancer (2011).

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1aku
documentation:
Binding of Flavin mononucleotido with D.Vulgaris(1aku)
Reference:  McCarthy et al. Crystallographic Investigation of the Role of Aspartate 95 in the Modulation of the
Redox Potentials of DesulfoVibrio Vulgaris Flavodoxin.(2002)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4pjt
documentation:
Binding of BMN 673 to catPARP1(4pj7)
Reference:  Aoyagi-Scharber et al. Structural basis for the inhibition of poly(ADP-ribose) polymerases 1 and 2 by BMN
673, a potent inhibitor derived from dihydropyridophthalazinone.(2014)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1bju
documentation:
Binding of ACPU to bovine tripsin(1bju)
Reference:  Presnell et al. Oxyanion-Mediated Inhibition of Serine Proteases.(1998)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4agl
documentation:
Binding of P53 to PhiKan784(4agl)
Reference:  Wilcken et al. Halogen-Enriched Fragment Libraries as Leads for Drug Rescue of Mutant p53.(2012)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_2efj
documentation:
Binding of teobromine to 1,7 dimethylxanthine methyltransferase(2efj)
Reference:  McCarthy et al. The Structure of Two N-Methyltransferases from the Caffeine Biosynthetic
Pathway.(2007)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_2iuz
documentation:
Binding of C2-dicaffeine to Aspergilius fumigates(2iuz)
Reference:  Schüttelkopf et al. Screening-based discovery and structural dissection of a novel family 18 chitinase
inhibitor.(2006)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3shy
documentation:
Binding of 5FO to PDE5A1 catalytic domain(3shy)
Reference:  Xu et al. Utilization of halogen bond in lead optimization: A case study of rational design of potent
phosphodiesterase type 5 (PDE5) inhibitors.(2011)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1ay8
documentation:
Binding of PLP to aromatic amino acid aminotransferase(1ay8)
Reference:  Okamoto et al. Crystal structures of Paracoccus denitrificans aromatic amino acid aminotransferase: a
substrate recognition site constructed by rearrangement of hydrogen bond network..(1998)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_4rdl
documentation:
Binding of Norovirus Boxer P domain with Lewis y tetrasaccharide(4rdl)
Reference:  Hao et al. Crystal structures of GI.8 Boxer virus P dimers in complex with HBGAs, a novel
evolutionary path selected by the Lewis epitope..(2014)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1hii
documentation:
HIV-2 protease in complex with novel inhibitor CGP 53820 (1hii)
Reference:  Comparative analysis of the X-ray structures of HIV-1 and HIV-2 proteases in complex
with CGP 53820, a novel pseudosymmetric inhibitor (1995)
Additional literature-validated cases #

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1hvi
documentation:
HIV-1 protease in complex with Diol inhibitor (1hvi)
Reference: Influence of Stereochemistry on Activity and Binding Modes for C2 Symmetry-Based
 Diol Inhibitors of HIV-1 Protease (1994)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_3o7g
documentation:
Inhibitor PLX4032 binding to B-RAF(V600E) (3og7)
Reference: Clinical efficacy of a RAF inhibitor needs broad target blockade in BRAF-mutant
melanoma (2010)

methods.name: plip.test.test_literature_validated.LiteratureValidatedTest.test_1hpx
documentation:
HIV-1 Protease complexes with the inhibitor KNI-272
Reference: Structure of HIV-1 protease with KNI-272, a tight-binding transition-state analog
containing allophenylnorstatine.
Note that the residue numbering is different in the publication and the PDB structure.
For residues in the B chain, the offset is -100 (e.g. Ile 50B in the PDB structure is Ile 150 in the paper).
Checks predictions against literature-validated interactions

functions.name: plip.basic.parallel.universal_worker
documentation:
This is a wrapper function expecting a tiplet of function, single
argument, dict of keyword arguments. The provided function is called
with the appropriate arguments.

functions.name: plip.basic.parallel.pool_args
documentation: Return a single iterator of n elements of lists of length 3, given a sequence of len n.

functions.name: plip.basic.parallel.parallel_fn
documentation:
Simple wrapper function, returning a parallel version of the given function f.
The function f must have one argument and may have an arbitray number of
keyword arguments.

variables.name: plip.basic.config.VERBOSE
documentation: Set verbose mode

variables.name: plip.basic.config.QUIET
documentation: Set verbose mode

variables.name: plip.basic.config.SILENT
documentation: Set verbose mode

variables.name: plip.basic.config.MAXTHREADS
documentation: Maximum number of main threads for binding site visualization

variables.name: plip.basic.config.RAWSTRING
documentation: use raw strings for input / output

variables.name: plip.basic.config.BREAKCOMPOSITE
documentation: Break up composite ligands with covalent bonds

variables.name: plip.basic.config.ALTLOC
documentation: Consider alternate locations

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PLUGIN_MODE
documentation: Special mode for PLIP in Plugins (e.g. PyMOL)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.NOFIX
documentation: Turn off fixing of errors in PDB files

variables.name: plip.basic.config.NOFIXFILE
documentation: Turn off writing to files for fixed PDB structures

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PEPTIDES
documentation: Definition which chains should be considered as peptide ligands

variables.name: plip.basic.config.OUTPUTFILENAME
documentation: Naming for the TXT and XML report files

variables.name: plip.basic.config.NOPDBCANMAP
documentation: Skip calculation of mapping canonical atom order: PDB atom order

variables.name: plip.basic.config.NOHYDRO
documentation: Do not add hydrogen bonds (in case already present in the structure)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.MODEL
documentation: The model to be selected for multi-model structures (default = 1).

variables.name: plip.basic.config.BS_DIST
documentation: Determines maximum distance to include binding site residues

variables.name: plip.basic.config.AROMATIC_PLANARITY
documentation: Determines allowed deviation from planarity in aromatic rings

variables.name: plip.basic.config.MIN_DIST
documentation: Minimum distance for all distance thresholds

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HYDROPH_DIST_MAX
documentation: Distance cutoff for detection of hydrophobic contacts

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HBOND_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance between hydrogen bond donor and acceptor (Hubbard & Haider, 2001) + 0.6 A

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HBOND_DON_ANGLE_MIN
documentation: Min. angle at the hydrogen bond donor (Hubbard & Haider, 2001) + 10

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PISTACK_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance for parallel or offset pistacking (McGaughey, 1998)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PISTACK_ANG_DEV
documentation: Max. Deviation from parallel or perpendicular orientation (in degrees)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PISTACK_OFFSET_MAX
documentation: Maximum offset of the two rings (corresponds to the radius of benzene + 0.5 A)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.PICATION_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance between charged atom and aromatic ring center (Gallivan and Dougherty, 1999)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.SALTBRIDGE_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance between centers of charge for salt bridges (Barlow and Thornton, 1983) + 1.5

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HALOGEN_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance between oxy. and halogen (Halogen bonds in biological molecules., Auffinger)+0.5

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HALOGEN_ACC_ANGLE
documentation: Optimal acceptor angle (Halogen bonds in biological molecules., Auffinger)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HALOGEN_DON_ANGLE
documentation: Optimal donor angle (Halogen bonds in biological molecules., Auffinger)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.HALOGEN_ANGLE_DEV
documentation: Max. deviation from optimal angle

variables.name: plip.basic.config.WATER_BRIDGE_MINDIST
documentation: Min. distance between water oxygen and polar atom (Jiang et al., 2005) -0.1

variables.name: plip.basic.config.WATER_BRIDGE_MAXDIST
documentation: Max. distance between water oxygen and polar atom (Jiang et al., 2005) +0.5

variables.name: plip.basic.config.WATER_BRIDGE_OMEGA_MIN
documentation: Min. angle between acceptor, water oxygen and donor hydrogen (Jiang et al., 2005) - 9

variables.name: plip.basic.config.WATER_BRIDGE_OMEGA_MAX
documentation: Max. angle between acceptor, water oxygen and donor hydrogen (Jiang et al., 2005)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.WATER_BRIDGE_THETA_MIN
documentation: Min. angle between water oxygen, donor hydrogen and donor atom (Jiang et al., 2005)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.METAL_DIST_MAX
documentation: Max. distance between metal ion and interacting atom (Harding, 2001)

variables.name: plip.basic.config.MAX_COMPOSITE_LENGTH
documentation: Filter out ligands with more than 200 fragments

classes.name: plip.basic.remote.VisualizerData
documentation: Contains all information on a complex relevant for visualization. Can be pickled

modules.name: plip.plipcmd
documentation:
Protein-Ligand Interaction Profiler - Analyze and visualize protein-ligand interactions in PDB files.
plipcmd.py - Main script for PLIP command line execution.
! /usr/bin/env python

functions.name: plip.plipcmd.process_pdb
documentation: Analysis of a single PDB file. Can generate textual reports XML, PyMOL session files and images as output.

functions.name: plip.plipcmd.download_structure
documentation:
Given a PDB ID, downloads the corresponding PDB structure.
Checks for validity of ID and handles error while downloading.
Returns the path of the downloaded file.

functions.name: plip.plipcmd.remove_duplicates
documentation:
Checks input lists for duplicates and returns
a list with unique entries

functions.name: plip.plipcmd.run_analysis
documentation: Main function. Calls functions for processing, report generation and visualization.

functions.name: plip.plipcmd.main
documentation: Parse command line arguments and start main script for analysis.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.tmpfile
documentation: Returns the path to a newly created temporary file.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.is_lig
documentation: Checks if a PDB compound can be excluded as a small molecule ligand

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.extract_pdbid
documentation: Use regular expressions to get a PDB ID from a string

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.whichrestype
documentation: Returns the residue name of an Pybel or OpenBabel atom.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.whichresnumber
documentation: Returns the residue number of an Pybel or OpenBabel atom (numbering as in original PDB file).

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.whichchain
documentation: Returns the residue number of an PyBel or OpenBabel atom.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.euclidean3d
documentation: Faster implementation of euclidean distance for the 3D case.
Mathematical operations

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.vector
documentation:
Vector from p1 to p2.
\:param p1: coordinates of point p1
\:param p2: coordinates of point p2
\:returns : numpy array with vector coordinates

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.vecangle
documentation:
Calculate the angle between two vectors
\:param v1: coordinates of vector v1
\:param v2: coordinates of vector v2
\:param deg: whether to return degrees or radians
\:returns : angle in degree or rad

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.normalize_vector
documentation:
Take a vector and return the normalized vector
\:param v: a vector v
\:returns : normalized vector v

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.centroid
documentation:
Calculates the centroid from a 3D point cloud and returns the coordinates
\:param coo: Array of coordinate arrays
\:returns : centroid coordinates as list

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.projection
documentation:
Calculates the centroid from a 3D point cloud and returns the coordinates
\:param pnormal1: normal of plane
\:param ppoint: coordinates of point in the plane
\:param tpoint: coordinates of point to be projected
\:returns : coordinates of point orthogonally projected on the plane

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.cluster_doubles
documentation:
Given a list of doubles, they are clustered if they share one element
\:param double_list: list of doubles
\:returns : list of clusters (tuples)

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.tilde_expansion
documentation: Tilde expansion, i.e. converts '~' in paths into <value of $HOME>.
File operations

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.folder_exists
documentation: Checks if a folder exists

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.create_folder_if_not_exists
documentation: Creates a folder if it does not exists.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.initialize_pymol
documentation: Initializes PyMOL
PyMOL-specific

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.start_pymol
documentation:
Starts up PyMOL and sets general options. Quiet mode suppresses all PyMOL output.
Command line options can be passed as the second argument.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.nucleotide_linkage
documentation: Support for DNA/RNA ligands by finding missing covalent linkages to stitch DNA/RNA together.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.ring_is_planar
documentation:
Given a set of ring atoms, check if the ring is sufficiently planar
to be considered aromatic

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.classify_by_name
documentation: Classify a (composite) ligand by the HETID(s)

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.sort_members_by_importance
documentation:
Sort the members of a composite ligand according to two criteria:
1. Split up in main and ion group. Ion groups are located behind the main group.
2. Within each group, sort by chain and position.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.get_isomorphisms
documentation: Get all isomorphisms of the ligand.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.canonicalize
documentation: Get the canonical atom order for the ligand.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.int32_to_negative
documentation:
Checks if a suspicious number (e.g. ligand position) is in fact a negative number represented as a
32 bit integer and returns the actual number.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.read_pdb
documentation: Reads a given PDB file and returns a Pybel Molecule.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.read
documentation: Returns a file handler and detects gzipped files.

functions.name: plip.basic.supplemental.readmol
documentation:
Reads the given molecule file and returns the corresponding Pybel molecule as well as the input file type.
In contrast to the standard Pybel implementation, the file is closed properly.

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.filter_contacts
documentation:
Filter interactions by two criteria:
1. No interactions between the same residue (important for intra mode).
2. No duplicate interactions (A with B and B with A, also important for intra mode).

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.hydrophobic_interactions
documentation:
Detection of hydrophobic pliprofiler between atom_set_a (binding site) and atom_set_b (ligand).
Definition: All pairs of qualified carbon atoms within a distance of HYDROPH_DIST_MAX
FUNCTIONS FOR DETECTION OF SPECIFIC INTERACTIONS

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.hbonds
documentation:
Detection of hydrogen bonds between sets of acceptors and donor pairs.
Definition: All pairs of hydrogen bond acceptor and donors with
donor hydrogens and acceptor showing a distance within HBOND DIST MIN and HBOND DIST MAX
and donor angles above HBOND_DON_ANGLE_MIN

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.pistacking
documentation: Return all pi-stackings between the given aromatic ring systems in receptor and ligand.

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.pication
documentation:
Return all pi-Cation interaction between aromatic rings and positively charged groups.
For tertiary and quaternary amines, check also the angle between the ring and the nitrogen.

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.saltbridge
documentation: Detect all salt bridges (pliprofiler between centers of positive and negative charge)

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.halogen
documentation: Detect all halogen bonds of the type Y-O...X-C

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.water_bridges
documentation: Find water-bridged hydrogen bonds between ligand and protein. For now only considers bridged of first degree.

functions.name: plip.structure.detection.metal_complexation
documentation: Find all metal complexes between metals and appropriate groups in both protein and ligand, as well as water

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBParser.parse_pdb
documentation:
Extracts additional information from PDB files.
I. When reading in a PDB file, OpenBabel numbers ATOMS and HETATOMS continously.
In PDB files, TER records are also counted, leading to a different numbering system.
This functions reads in a PDB file and provides a mapping as a dictionary.
II. Additionally, it returns a list of modified residues.
III. Furthermore, covalent linkages between ligands and protein residues/other ligands are identified
IV. Alternative conformations

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBParser.fix_pdbline
documentation: Fix a PDB line if information is missing.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBParser.get_linkage
documentation: Get the linkage information from a LINK entry PDB line.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.getpeptides
documentation:
If peptide ligand chains are defined via the command line options,
try to extract the underlying ligand formed by all residues in the
given chain without water

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.getligs
documentation:
Get all ligands from a PDB file and prepare them for analysis.
Returns all non-empty ligands.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.extract_ligand
documentation: Extract the ligand by copying atoms and bonds and assign all information necessary for later steps.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.is_het_residue
documentation:
Given an OBResidue, determines if the residue is indeed a possible ligand
in the PDB file

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.filter_for_ligands
documentation: Given an OpenBabel Molecule, get all ligands, their names, and water

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.LigandFinder.identify_kmers
documentation: Using the covalent linkage information, find out which fragments/subunits form a ligand.

classes.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mapper

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mapper.id_to_atom
documentation:
Returns the atom for a given original ligand ID.
To do this, the ID is mapped to the protein first and then the atom returned.
Provides functions for mapping atom IDs in the correct way

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mol.hydrophobic_atoms
documentation: Select all carbon atoms which have only carbons and/or hydrogens as direct neighbors.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mol.find_hba
documentation: Find all possible hydrogen bond acceptors

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mol.find_hbd
documentation: Find all possible strong and weak hydrogen bonds donors (all hydrophobic C-H pairings)

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Mol.find_rings
documentation:
Find rings and return only aromatic.
Rings have to be sufficiently planar OR be detected by OpenBabel as aromatic.

classes.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.__init__
documentation: Detect all interactions when initializing

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.find_unpaired_ligand
documentation: Identify unpaired functional in groups in ligands, involving H-Bond donors, acceptors, halogen bond donors.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.refine_hydrophobic
documentation: Apply several rules to reduce the number of hydrophobic interactions.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.refine_hbonds_ldon
documentation: Refine selection of hydrogen bonds. Do not allow groups which already form salt bridges to form H-Bonds.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.refine_hbonds_pdon
documentation:
Refine selection of hydrogen bonds. Do not allow groups which already form salt bridges to form H-Bonds with
atoms of the same group.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.refine_pication
documentation:
Just important for constellations with histidine involved. If the histidine ring is positioned in stacking
position to an aromatic ring in the ligand, there is in most cases stacking and pi-cation interaction reported
as histidine also carries a positive charge in the ring. For such cases, only report stacking.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PLInteraction.refine_water_bridges
documentation:
A donor atom already forming a hydrogen bond is not allowed to form a water bridge. Each water molecule
can only be donor for two water bridges, selecting the constellation with the omega angle closest to 110 deg.
Class to store a ligand, a protein and their interactions.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.BindingSite.__init__
documentation: Find all relevant parts which could take part in interactions

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.BindingSite.find_hal
documentation: Look for halogen bond acceptors (Y-{O|P|N|S}, with Y=C,P,S)

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.BindingSite.find_charged
documentation: Looks for positive charges in arginine, histidine or lysine, for negative in aspartic and glutamic acid.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.BindingSite.find_metal_binding
documentation:
Looks for atoms that could possibly be involved in chelating a metal ion.
This can be any main chain oxygen atom or oxygen, nitrogen and sulfur from specific amino acids

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Ligand.get_canonical_num
documentation: Converts internal atom ID into canonical atom ID. Agrees with Canonical SMILES in XML.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Ligand.is_functional_group
documentation: Given a pybel atom, look up if it belongs to a function group

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Ligand.find_hal
documentation: Look for halogen bond donors (X-C, with X=F, Cl, Br, I)

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Ligand.find_charged
documentation:
Identify all positively charged groups in a ligand. This search is not exhaustive, as the cases can be quite
diverse. The typical cases seem to be protonated amines, quaternary ammoinium and sulfonium
as mentioned in 'Cation-pi interactions in ligand recognition and catalysis' (Zacharias et al., 2002)).
Identify negatively charged groups in the ligand.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.Ligand.find_metal_binding

classes.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex.load_pdb
documentation:
Loads a pdb file with protein AND ligand(s), separates and prepares them.
If specified 'as_string', the input is a PDB string instead of a path.

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex.analyze
documentation: Triggers analysis of all complexes in structure

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex.characterize_complex
documentation: Handles all basic functions for characterizing the interactions for one ligand

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex.extract_bs
documentation: Return list of ids from residues belonging to the binding site

methods.name: plip.structure.preparation.PDBComplex.res_belongs_to_bs
documentation:
Check for each residue if its centroid is within a certain distance to the ligand centroid.
Additionally checks if a residue belongs to a chain restricted by the user (e.g. by defining a peptide chain)

Contains a collection of objects associated with a PDB complex, i.e. one or several ligands and their binding
sites as well as information about the pliprofiler between them. Provides functions to load and prepare input files
such as PDB files.
